name: Opening keynote: The agentic cloud
description: The Next '26 Opening Keynote introduces the blueprint for the agentic enterprise, guiding businesses through the shift from AI adoption to large-scale transformation. Join Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian for major product announcements and hear from global customers who are partnering with Google Cloud to drive real-world impact.

speakers.jobTitle: SVP and Chief Technologist, AI and Infrastructure
COO, Google Cloud and President, Security Products

name: NVIDIA Omniverse for physical AI: From simulation to reality

custom_fields.c_92134: Partner Innovation, Technology & Leadership, Customer Story

description: Digital twins and real-time simulation are reshaping industries in the era of physical AI. In this session, we’ll explore how digital twins are accelerating innovation across product design, manufacturing, robotics, autonomous vehicles, smart cities, and more. Attendees will learn how teams are deploying NVIDIA Omniverse and NVIDIA Isaac Sim on Google Cloud using Google G4 instances, powered by RTX Pro 6000 GPUs, to train AI models, test autonomous systems, and optimize operations before real-world deployment. Learn how developers and enterprises are running these simulation and digital twin workloads on Google Cloud today. Discover how OpenUSD-based workflows and GPU-accelerated computing are shortening development cycles, reducing costs, and de-risking deployment across physical AI applications. This Session is hosted by a Google Cloud Next Sponsor. Visit your registration profile at g.co/cloudnext to opt out of sharing your contact information with the sponsor hosting this session.

name: The journey to agentic applications at Spotify

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, Data Analysts, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins, IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: Spotify is actively deploying agentic applications in their cloud infrastructure. Spotify will share their goals and discuss the top of mind infrastructure considerations that have shaped their journey to support agentic applications. In this talk Spotify will share their experience in adopting MCP in their gRPC environment, implementing AI guardrails and managing multitiered identity for agent interactions. Spotify is exploring model serving optimization with inference gateway extensions and across a wide range of runtime form factors spanning ARM, GPU and TPU. Spotify is building this support on top of a highly reliable regionalized network architecture to which they have recently migrated their entire cloud deployment.

name: Multi-Agent Systems: Preventing Chaos in Enterprise Production

custom_fields.c_92133: IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: Enterprise AI is shifting from single models to multi-agent systems (MAS), but production is where ambition meets reality. At scale, agents can conflict, hallucinate, or fail silently. This talk shares lessons from deploying MAS in global enterprises, covering intelligent control planes, governance guardrails, real-world reliability, new decisioning systems and operating models. It explores practical patterns for observability, graceful degradation, and human in the loop escalation to accelerate business value without sacrificing trust. This Session is hosted by a Google Cloud Next Sponsor. Visit your registration profile at g.co/cloudnext to opt out of sharing your contact information with the sponsor hosting this session.

speakers.jobTitle: VP, North America Data and AI Head

name: Beyond the simple SELECT: Become an analytics hero with AlloyDB NL2SQL
description: Writing a simple SELECT is easy but crafting complex joins to get the exact result you want is not. To make matters worse, you can't write effective queries without spending hours understanding your data schema. Let me present the AlloyDB NL2SQL extension. In this session, discover how AlloyDB builds critical layers of metadata that help AI models understand your data context. Learn how to turn natural language into correct SQL execution and become an analytics hero in minutes, not days.

custom_fields.c_92133: Executive, IT Managers & Business Leaders

This session moves beyond the code to explore how Mattel integrates Google Cloud AI with Jira to automate complex workflows and drive organizational efficiency. Discover the blueprint for building agents that engineers trust and how this architecture scales to support broader quality goals. This Session is hosted by a Google Cloud Next Sponsor. Visit your registration profile at g.co/cloudnext to opt out of sharing your contact information with the sponsor hosting this session.

speakers.jobTitle: Chief Safety Officer, SVP Global Quality, Product Safety, and Regulatory Compliance

name: Architecting Gemini Enterprise for regulated industries
description: "Can we use GenAI without data leaving our perimeter?" For regulated industries, the answer must be a verifiable "Yes." This session whiteboards a Zero Trust GenAI Architecture using Gemini. We design the "Private RAG" pattern for confidential data, tackling three challenges: Perimeter, by configuring VPC-SC and PSC to block public internet traversal; Encryption, via CMEK and Data Residency for sovereignty; and Identity, by architecting ACL propagation to the Vector Database. Leave with a reference architecture that satisfies your CISO.

speakers.jobTitle: Technical Solutions Consultant, Office of the CISO

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, IT Managers & Business Leaders

Discover how this collaboration eliminates tooling fragmentation, allowing teams to monitor AI agents with the same rigor as traditional software. The session will also explore how this standardization enables Arize to ingest GCP telemetry directly, enriching the Arize AX experience and providing developers with a seamless, end-to-end observability solution for AI agents running on Google Cloud.

Join this session to explore how Google is securing AI agents, and how these capabilities are rebuilding the foundation of trust for the next generation of cloud computing.

The time for mere assistance is over, and the era of autonomous action is here.

name: Automating excellence: How Gemini and Config Connector help create 10x cloud teams

description: Operations are the primary bottleneck in hyperscale cloud environments. Small teams often struggle to manage massive Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) footprints, drowning in manual toil and configuration drift. The solution lies in a practical architecture for the “10x cloud team.” In this session, we’ll demonstrate how combining Config Connector with Gemini 1.5 Pro manages Google Cloud resources as native Kubernetes objects, bridging the gap between intent and implementation. Learn to use Config Connector reconciliation loops and Gemini to resolve drift and refactor technical debt in real time. Leave with a reference architecture for an AI-augmented Kubernetes control plane.

speakers.jobTitle: COO, Google Cloud and President, Security Products

name: Building multi-agent orchestration: Key challenges and solution approaches
description: A single agent is useful, but coordinated teams are game-changers. Scaling multi-agent systems shifts the challenge from prompting to complex orchestration. This deep dive explores managing state, error handling, and task routing in production. We compare hierarchical, graph-based, and broadcast architectures, sharing battle-tested patterns for Google Cloud. Learn to implement robust recovery for chain failures, design efficient agent-to-agent communication, and secure tool-use via Vertex AI and Gemini for enterprise-scale deployment.

name: Zero to scale: Rapid development with Google Cloud & MCP Toolbox for database

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, Database Professionals

We explore how the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Google's open-source MCP Toolbox give agents real-time, grounded access to AlloyDB, BigQuery, and Spanner through simple YAML tool definitions. See two live demos: Prism, a multi-database retail agent that routes natural language to the right database; and a DB Builder agent that designs and extends full database backends from a plain English description.

name: Building an AI app: A low-code guide for small teams

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, Database Professionals, IT Managers & Business Leaders

name: Build multi-agent systems that actually work

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: Multi-agent architectures are powerful but over-applied. Join this session with Anthropic to learn when multiple agents genuinely outperform a single agent – and how to build them with Claude on Vertex AI. You’ll gain insight into the three scenarios where multi-agent consistently wins (context isolation, parallel execution, and specialization), the four architecture patterns with concrete trade-offs, and the context-centric decomposition strategy most teams get wrong. Plus, you’ll explore verification subagents and the pitfalls that undermine even well-designed systems. This Session is hosted by a Google Cloud Next Sponsor. name: Long-Running Agents: The AI that never sleeps

custom_fields.c_79385: Agents, App Dev, Applied AI, Architecture, Serverless, Vertex AI

description: Today's AI agents are impressive but often don't run for long - they forget context mid-task, hallucinate when sessions get long, and declare victory before the job is done. What if an agent could work autonomously for hours or even days, maintaining state, recovering from failures, and actually shipping results? In this session, we'll explore the architecture and patterns behind long-running agents — persistent AI workers that go far beyond single-prompt interactions. Through two live demos built on Google ADK and Cloud Run, you'll see a 24/7 competitive intelligence agent that monitors competitors across Reddit, YouTube, Hacker News, and the web (with historical trend detection powered by Firestore), and a creative coding agent that transformed a large Blender scene into a browser-explorable 3D environment in a single continuous session. We'll cover the key engineering breakthroughs that make this possible — agent harnesses, persistent memory patterns, self-verification loops, and how Agent Engine's Sessions and Memory Bank provide managed infrastructure for agent persistence at scale. You'll leave with practical patterns for building agents that don't just answer questions, but plan, execute, debug, iterate, and ship.

description: This session explores the rise of GenMedia as a core pillar of modern film/movie/media production, offering a deep dive into how Google’s Generative AI models are being deployed at scale to help creators realize previously impossible cinematic visions. Our analysis focuses on the engineering of the frame, specifically detailing the specialized generative super-resolution and outpainting techniques used to intelligently widen aspect ratios and upscale source material. By synthesizing contextually accurate visual data, these tools allow filmmakers to expand their original narrative canvas into larger and wider canvases without compromising the original cinematography or narrative integrity. Attendees will gain insight into how Google’s unique AI infrastructure enables a truly next-level viewing experience, scaling pixels and perspectives to meet the rigorous demands of the world's most advanced digital theaters and premium large-format screens.

name: Building AI-Native Data Platform: Automating the Lifecycle from Analysis to Modeling to Engineering

This architecture creates a powerful innovation flywheel: as the platform automates its own engineering and operational rigors, it accelerates the delivery of new AI use cases, which in turn feeds more metadata back into the system to drive even faster, more intelligent deployments. Witness a revolutionary blueprint in which AI-native agents not only automate the building of Data Vault but also proactively manage the “Day 2” health of Data Vault, delivering a truly end-to-end, hands-free experience.

name: Agentic FinOps for BigQuery cost optimization

Last, run a vector search on product images stored in Cloud Storage to identify the most similar products to your search terms based on image features and metadata.

custom_fields.c_92133: Data Analysts, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, Application Developers, IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: Apps are becoming adaptive systems that respond in real time. But AI cannot adapt to what it cannot see. While logs track events, they miss the human struggle on the screen. Fullstory provides the digital sight that reveals true user intent. Learn how to turn behavioral data into a proactive AI input, moving from analyzing yesterday’s friction to orchestrating intelligent digital experiences. Grounding AI in human context allows you to close the loop for a self-improving journey at AI speed. By attending this session, your contact information may be shared with the sponsor for relevant follow up for this event only.

name: Foundation model for AgriTech
description: In this session, you'll discover how to fine-tune domain-specific multimodal models to drive real-world results. We will demonstrate how Gemma3, PaliGemma2, and Gemini work together to power crop disease detection and interactive farmer Q&A. By integrating these models with Vertex AI, you'll learn how to tackle complex agricultural challenges head-on. Together, we will whiteboard a scalable AgriTech Foundation Model architecture, equipping you with practical design patterns to deploy industry-specific solutions and transform cutting-edge research into high-value applications.

name: The agent governance pattern: Architecting a secure enterprise AI workforce
description: As organizations move from AI pilots to "Agent Ecosystems," ad-hoc security fails. How do you govern a non-human workforce operating across fragmented platforms? This technical session whiteboards a decoupled governance architecture—a "single pane of glass" for your agent fleet. Join us to architect a solution that layers identity management, granular access control, and human-in-the-loop protocols. Ensure your Gemini and Vertex AI agents are as secure and auditable as your human workforce without sacrificing the pace of innovation.

name: Bridging worlds: Bringing Google Cloud Platform's power to open source data lakes
description: Migrate petabyte-scale Iceberg data lakes to Google Cloud Platform while retaining format benefits. This interactive session uses a real customer use case to evaluate Lakehouse architecture in GCP. Learn to design pipelines and blueprint an agentic data foundation. We will detail BigQuery/Lakehouse integration with Vertex AI and Gemini, and you will leave with an target blueprint to turn data into a foundation for gen AI and scale-optimized analytics.

name: 2026 M-Trends Report

custom_fields.c_92133: Security Professionals

description: Stay ahead of the latest cyberthreats with frontline insights from Mandiant incident response investigations. In this 17th edition of M-Trends, we share our observations and analysis of the dynamic threat landscape, and aim to provide you with actionable guidance to enhance your security posture.

name: Diagnose and optimize: A guide to ML workload observability on TPUs

custom_fields.c_92133: Infrastructure Architects & Admins

description: Diagnosing and optimizing ML workloads on TPUs is complicated and requires visibility from your ML workload all the way to the TPUs. In this discussion group, we'll introduce new capabilities to diagnose workloads on TPUs, including ML Diagnostics platform, Managed Xprof profiling, and Workload Monitoring. These capabilities from internal Google platforms such as Gemini, Search, and Youtube are useful for their workload diagnosis on TPUs. Join this deep dive to learn how these tools can make your workloads run best on TPUs.

name: Securing AI-Generated Code: What's Actually Working?

custom_fields.c_92133: Security Professionals

description: AI coding tools have fundamentally changed how software gets built. Developers are prompting instead of coding. Agents are committing autonomously. Code volume is accelerating faster than any security team can manually review. But most software security programs were designed for a world where humans wrote every line. In this interactive discussion, we'll have an honest conversation about how to maintain visibility, prioritize what actually matters, and what a modern AppSec program should look like. Come ready to share what's working, what isn't, and what you're still figuring out.

name: Deep dive into building apps to cloud directly from your IDE

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins

description: Vibe coding provides velocity, but operationalizing intent-driven applications with enterprise-grade governance remains a significant architectural challenge. This discussion group will dive deep into the technical mechanics of using Gemini Cloud Assist agents to bridge the gap between source code and compliant infrastructure. We'll analyze the workflow of publishing approved module catalogs and how agentic logic automates identity and access management policies and CI/CD pipelines.

name: Threat hunters unite: A discussion on Agentic defense

custom_fields.c_92133: Security Professionals

description: Join this discussion group to learn ways to generate detection rules based on emerging threats. Come prepared to share insights with peers in a smaller more collaborative setting.

name: AI inference: Performance when you need it, economy when you don't

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins

description: As Generative AI moves from pilot to production, the 'one-size-fits-all' approach to serving is no longer viable. Platform teams face the dual challenge of meeting stringent SLOs for real-time applications while minimizing TCO for high-volume, cost-sensitive tasks. In this session, we unveil GKE Inference Gateway innovations designed to solve this equation. Explore how GKE delivers multi-accelerator flexibility and disaggregated serving for optimal price-performance. We’ll demonstrate how to fortify models using Model Armor, agent identity, and token-based quota protections. Finally, discover how to optimize inference with accelerators spread across multiple cloud regions

name: BigQuery multimodal embeddings and vector search
description: Join this session to brainstorm how multimodal vector search unlocks the true meaning of your data. We will explore moving beyond simple text matching to perform similarity searches on images, audio, and video using Google's ScaNN technology. The group will discuss building serverless indexes that scale to billions of embeddings. We will examine how to integrate these semantic insights directly with analytics to power recommendation engines, entity resolution, and personalized experiences.

name: Cross-Cloud Network: An intelligent, governed, performant fabric for global AI

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins, IT Managers & Business Leaders, Security Professionals

description: As AI evolves from stand-alone models to autonomous agents, the network becomes the critical integration layer. This session will introduce new capabilities, delivering centralized governance and observability to securely connect agents, models, and tools everywhere – all underpinned by Google’s planet-scale Cross-Cloud Network. Discover expansions to Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) Inference Gateway and eliminate compute silos by intelligently pooling traffic to accelerator capacity, anywhere. We’ll explore the high-performance foundation that makes it all possible: AI-optimized Virtual Private Cloud and GKE networking, engineered to eliminate bottlenecks in massive model training and inference.

name: Boost AI context with hybrid search in Spanner

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, Data Analysts, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, Database Professionals, Infrastructure Architects & Admins, IT Managers & Business Leaders

We’ll unveil new AI innovations that simplify contextual search and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) applications. name: Secure your resources with built-in Policy, Policy Intelligence, and PAM

custom_fields.c_92133: Security Professionals

See how Policy Intelligence, through tools like IAM Recommender and Policy Analyzer, helps right-size permissions, while Privileged Access Manager (PAM) enables secure, time-bound elevated access. Leave with actionable strategies to enhance your cloud security using built-in Policy, PAM, and Policy Intelligence.

By adopting Google Cloud Observability, they reduced operational costs, simplified metrics management, and aligned their observability practices with open source OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, and Grafana standards.

Mayes will demonstrate Web AI solutions, using LiteRT.js to create a vector search system with minimal effort while maintaining control. And Astrum will cover creating reusable skills that your team can lean on to scale out productivity gains.

custom_fields.c_92133: IT Managers & Business Leaders, Security Professionals

This session will focus on simplifying security operations by moving from fragmented tools to unified, automated protection. We’ll demonstrate how to use agentic threat intelligence to streamline threat hunting, significantly reducing the burden on your staff. Shift your posture from reactive to proactive with practical guidance on turning insights into immediate action – achieving enterprise-grade security with the simplicity your team needs to innovate and move fast.

name: Unlocking enterprise actions: Bridge your APIs to AI agents with MCP and Apigee

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers

description: As generative AI moves from conversation to execution, your agents need reliable access to enterprise capabilities. In this session, discover how to transform your existing API landscape into an agent-ready ecosystem using Apigee. We’ll demonstrate how to instantly create Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers from existing enterprise APIs and “boost” API specifications to ensure high-accuracy consumption by LLMs. Join us to learn advanced techniques for generating specifications directly from API logs, registering third-party MCPs, and securing your MCP servers with robust policies. And find out how Apigee enables your agents to utilize enterprise capabilities securely and at scale.

name: Achieve state-of-the-art inference: High performance on TPUs and GPUs with llm-d

custom_fields.c_92133: DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins, IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: Proprietary stacks and generic open source solutions often lack deep hardware integration. Break free from proprietary constraints with llm-d, an open source stack that delivers state-of-the-art performance across Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) and NVIDIA GPUs. This session dives deep into how to architect disaggregated serving and automatic key-value cache storage tiering on Ironwood (TPU7x). Learn to implement routing optimized for service-level objectives and build a portable, high-performance inference fleet that scales automatically based on real-time server conditions. Leave with a reference architecture for hardware-optimized LLM serving.

name: Cloud Storage Rapid: Turbocharged object storage for AI and analytics

custom_fields.c_92133: Infrastructure Architects & Admins

description: Traditional object storage is built for scale, not speed. Why not have both? Cloud Storage Rapid is a high-performance tier of object storage built for intensive workloads like data preparation, training, and inference. Used by some of the world’s largest AI and ML customers, Cloud Storage Rapid (including Anywhere Cache and Rapid Bucket) provides terabyte-scale throughput and submillisecond latency while maintaining simplicity, scale, and cost-effectiveness. Join us to dissect real-world architectures where feeding compute clusters directly from Google Cloud Storage maximizes graphics processing unit, tensor processing unit, and data management utilization while reducing overall complexity.

Discover strategies driving their transformative results like 70% faster media activation and 40% higher ROI, by using Google Cloud to close the insight-to-activation gap.

description: Building the future of Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) requires architecting the same agentic workflows we provide to our customers. Discover how we build and scale these discovery agents on GKE, integrating real-time feedback loops and kernel-level isolation. We’ll deconstruct this GKE-on-GKE pattern, providing battle-tested insights for your own agentic infrastructure on Google Cloud. Leave with a reference architecture and blueprint for building your own autonomous, self-optimizing platforms.

name: Data Validation at Scale: Architecting an AI-Native Validation Engine

Via a banking-grade demo, we showcase a code-first evaluation strategy (dfcx-scrapi) for metrics, agent-led self-escalation, and robust automated CI/CD to ensure trusted, human-in-the-loop AI.

We’ll demonstrate how to use Google Cloud’s G4 machine series and NVIDIA Omniverse to bridge the gap between virtual and physical worlds. Dive into the technical stack and learn how AI-driven stress-testing in simulation ensures reliability in production deployments.

name: Transform meetings into outcomes using Google Workspace with Gemini

custom_fields.c_92133: IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: Meetings should be engines of productivity, not administrative bottlenecks. In this session, discover how Gemini serves as a proactive partner to transform the meeting experience – from intelligent preparation to automated follow-up. We’ll demonstrate how to use Gemini to instantly synthesize agendas from your existing emails and files, capture real-time action items without distraction, and convert discussions into tangible next steps. Join us to learn how to minimize the logistics of collaboration and maximize the output of every meeting.

name: The BigQuery advantage: AI-powered migrations to an AI-ready data platform

custom_fields.c_92133: Data Analysts, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, Database Professionals, DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, IT Managers & Business Leaders
custom_fields.c_92134: Customer Story, Partner Innovation, Technology & Leadership

description: Migrating to the cloud is no longer just about reducing costs – it’s about unlocking business value with AI. This session provides a blueprint for moving your critical data workloads to BigQuery with unprecedented speed. Discover how to eliminate infrastructure management, lower your total cost of ownership (TCO), and prepare your data foundation for the next wave of AI capabilities. Join us to learn how AI-powered BigQuery Migration Services automates the migration journey, de-risking your migration and turning a multiyear roadmap into a fast track for innovation.

speakers.jobTitle: SVP of Product Strategy, Open Intelligence

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, Data Analysts, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, Database Professionals, Infrastructure Architects & Admins, IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: Learn how to build and operate a zero-ETL low latency serving platform on Spanner that’s natively integrated with your Apache Iceberg data lakehouses such as BigQuery. Discover how the Spanner columnar engine accelerates high-performance analytics on operational data. Explore seamless integrations with BigQuery, federated queries to data lakes, and high-performance Apache Spark jobs with Spanner Data Boost. Join this session to learn how to unify your entire data landscape, eliminating complex ETL pipelines and unlocking insights for your agentic AI and real-time analytics workloads.

speakers.jobTitle: AI Adoption and Data Platform Leader

name: Unified management plane for OTel: Server-side data control and processing

custom_fields.c_92133: DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs

description: OpenTelemetry (OTel) is the standard for observability, but enterprises often feel forced to choose between the reliability and scale of hyperscalers and the true open data models OpenTelemetry promises. In this session, we’ll introduce Telemetry Router, a managed solution that acts as the missing management plane for enterprise observability. Learn how to move beyond complex client-side management to achieve centralized data control, server-side transformation, and true data ownership. And take away a blueprint for scaling OTel without vendor lock-in.

name: Scale multimodal AI: Serverless data-parallel ML with Dataflow

custom_fields.c_92133: Data Analysts, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins, IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: Unstructured data is the fuel for modern multimodal AI, yet transforming massive volumes of images, audio, and video for inference presents a tremendous engineering challenge. In this session, we’ll demonstrate how Dataflow addresses this by offering a unified, serverless platform that democratizes data-parallel machine learning (ML) for high-performance feature engineering and inference. Learn how to use Dataflow to connect Cloud Storage and BigQuery, process millions of raw images, and power scalable embedding generation and vector search.

name: Transform cyber defense: Insights from Mandiant

custom_fields.c_92133: Security Professionals

description: Why do some security transformations succeed while others stall? We’ll look at the journey from legacy systems to modern security operations through the lens of those who have made the move. Come explore the common pitfalls and the breakthrough strategies that define a successful shift. We’ll discuss how to evolve your cyber defense functions to meet today’s threats. This session will set the stage for a deeper look into modern security, providing you with the vision and context needed to lead your organization into the next era of defense.

speakers.jobTitle: Business and Systems Resilience Security - Remote

name: The GKE inference playbook: Optimize cost and performance

custom_fields.c_92133: DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins, IT Managers & Business Leaders
custom_fields.c_92134: Customer Story, Small IT Teams, Sustainability, Technology & Leadership

description: Balancing inference latency and cost is generative AI’s toughest challenge. This session delivers the playbook for a high-performance, cost-efficient inference stack on Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE). Explore innovations for scaling state-of-the-art models, crushing cold starts, and maximizing utilization with Ironwood Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) and vLLM. We’ll take a deep dive into GKE Inference Gateway, demonstrating routing based on the service level objective (SLO) across clusters to ensure your application stays responsive – and your budget stays intact.

name: Enable autonomous data agents with BigQuery and Cloud Run

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, Data Analysts, Data Scientists, Data Engineers

description: Enterprise data is often trapped behind fragmented APIs and complex models, hindering AI agents from extracting insights. name: SRE at 5 bi-events: Predictive ops with BigQuery, Vertex AI & Gemini
description: How do you guarantee 2-second P99 latency across 5 billion daily events? Reactive alerting cannot keep up. This session unveils a GitOps-driven SRE framework built on Cloud Monitoring and BigQuery. We will demonstrate a live evolution of this architecture, moving beyond static dashboards to Predictive Operations. Watch as we combine BigQuery ML for SLO forecasting with Vertex AI to orchestrate Gemini for generative Root Cause Analysis. Attendees leave with a deployable blueprint and a hands-on lab to build their own AI-powered SRE Assistant.

name: 2026 M-Trends Report

description: Stay ahead of the latest cyberthreats with frontline insights from Mandiant incident response investigations. name: Lilly's AI catalyst: Streamlining the pharma tech transfer process

custom_fields.c_92133: Data Analysts, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, Executive, IT Managers & Business Leaders

AI-driven automation curates a knowledge base for instant visibility into procedures and lessons learned. Additionally, AI enhances Process Monitoring of Test Batch Data, providing insights and alerts to accelerate commercial production timelines.

name: Visualizing BigQuery Data with Looker Studio

In this hands-on workshop, you will create BigQuery views and connect them as data sources. You will then build a comprehensive report featuring date range controls and multiple charts. Learn to transform raw data into customized, shareable visualizations that effectively communicate the story behind the numbers.

name: Configure Vector Search in AlloyDB
description: Unlock semantic search with AlloyDB and Vertex AI. AlloyDB is a fully managed, PostgreSQL-compatible database service designed for your most demanding workloads. In this introductory-level workshop, you will implement vector search to find data based on meaning rather than exact keywords—ideal for complex inputs. You’ll learn to configure an AlloyDB database, load data, and generate text embeddings. Finally, you will execute fast similarity searches to identify related content, empowering your applications with AI-driven retrieval capabilities.

name: Securing the AI Era

custom_fields.c_92133: Security Professionals

Discover how agentic defense can help you stay ahead of threats and learn how to secure your digital transformation with confidence.

name: The agentic era: how AI is reshaping medical imaging discovery at scale

Optum and Google Cloud explore the shift from traditional search to agentic AI workflows. We’ll discuss how AI agents are bridging the expertise gap for imaging newcomers, accelerating interpretation through automated report generation, and streamlining triage. This session offers a vision for the future of imaging: where intelligent agents navigate petabytes of DICOM data to surface insights, driving faster outcomes and clinical efficiency.

name: Automate BigQuery optimization for continuous cost efficiency

name: ThreatSpace APT Hunting

Use Google Security Operations to determine all threat actor activities and use Google Threat Intelligence to provide attribution. Each day will align to a different APT, so join us to see if you can correctly identify all three APT threat actors. Laptops provided.

name: Deploying Applications with GKE Autopilot
description: Simplify operations with Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) Autopilot, a managed mode where Google creates, sizes, and scales your infrastructure automatically. In this introductory-level workshop, you will containerize an application and deploy it to an Autopilot cluster using standard Kubernetes commands. Learn to offload maintenance tasks like monitoring and capacity recalculation. Focus on building mission-critical apps while Google optimizes your cluster’s performance, security, and availability.

name: Stop Chasing Alerts: Fight Agentic Attacks with next-gen MSSPs

custom_fields.c_92133: Security Professionals

description: You don’t need to be a security expert to protect your organization against the next wave of threats. In this rapid-fire lightning talk, Google Cloud Security leaders will explore how AI-driven, agentic security operations are transforming customer outcomes. Discover how next-gen managed security service providers (MSSPs) leverage Google's agentic SOC to move beyond basic alert triage. Learn how to quickly implement these expert resources to fight AI with AI, drastically reduce alert fatigue, and remediate threats in real-time.

name: The priority paradox: Navigating multi-tenant AI on GKE
description: What happens when your high-priority RL training job and your mission-critical LLM inference spike hit the same cluster at the same time? Building a multi-tenant AI platform is a game of constant compromise. In this session, we’ll go beyond the "perfect" reference architecture to discuss the messy reality of multi-cluster orchestration. We'll share peer-to-peer insights on setting preemption policies that don’t alienate researchers, managing "goodput" during traffic surges, and the architectural shifts required to turn a collection of Kubernetes clusters into a unified, efficient AI factory.

name: Sovereign AI in action: Planning your Sovereign AI roadmap on Google Cloud

name: Build and orchestrate enterprise agents with Gemini and Atlassian Rovo

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, Data Analysts, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins, IT Managers & Business Leaders, Security Professionals

Join us to witness the Gemini CLI evolve into the universal connector, dispatching tasks to any agent – from custom agents to Atlassian Rovo. We’ll unveil the “agents everywhere” architecture with a live demo of the Agent2Agent (A2A) protocol. Observe the Gemini CLI act as a secure control plane, seamlessly routing tasks to your agents and to Rovo for expert execution. Discover how to build a unified, scalable agent network that breaks down silos between your tools and teams.

name: Accelerate large-scale model pretraining and reinforcement learning fine-tuning

custom_fields.c_92133: Data Analysts, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, Infrastructure Architects & Admins

speakers.jobTitle: Senior Staff Software Engineer, Machine Learning, Google Cloud Compute Infrastructure

name: Build beyond the CPU: Native AI and agentic autoscaling on GKE

custom_fields.c_92133: DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins

description: Standard autoscaling based on CPU and memory metrics often forces a choice: either over-provision resources or risk latency spikes during demand surges. Join this session for an introduction to intent-based autoscaling in Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE), a paradigm that adds automated rightsizing and custom workload metrics as native capabilities. Learn to architect native scaling based on application metrics such as queue depths, agent tool use, or latency histograms, eliminating the need for external adapters or sidecars. And discover how to move beyond generic resource limits to create a fluid infrastructure that reacts to real-time AI demand.

name: Accelerate CI/CD with coding agents

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins

description: Learn how generative AI transforms platform engineering through a zero-to-hero workflow. In this session, we’ll demonstrate how to use natural language to scaffold continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD) pipelines, manage Artifact Registry repositories, and streamline Identity and Access Management (IAM) authentication – all from the developer tools you already know and love. Discover how to automate containerization with Cloud Build and how to define promotion strategies in Cloud Deploy. Join us to learn how to trigger vulnerability scanning with simple prompts, and explore the architecture for translating prompts into Terraform.

name: Stop searching, start deciding: Accelerating business decisions with Gemini
description: Is your enterprise’s most valuable intelligence locked in disconnected silos? We’ll break down real-world customer use cases for onboarding complex datasets and deploying AI agents that cut through the noise. name: From prototype to production: Hard-won lessons for advanced, reliable, and secure agents

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers

Join us as some of our customers in production today share what to look out for and how each of them solved for agent quality, security, management, and reliability. We’ll also provide a retrospective of recurring failure patterns identified across more than 70 customer engagements to help you bridge the last-mile gap between a fast prototype and a trustworthy solution.

name: Manage incidents and maintenance for your Google Cloud operations

custom_fields.c_92133: DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: Incidents are inevitable in cloud operations, but chaos is a choice. This session is your guide to turning reactive alerts into proactive commands. Learn to harness Google Cloud’s health signals – Personalized Service Health for incidents and Unified Maintenance for scheduled maintenance – and integrate them into your workflows to cut through the noise. Learn how Gemini Cloud Assist investigates live health events to validate impact and scope. Get insights from customers who use these signals to manage incidents and maintenance, and leave with the playbook for building a truly resilient operation.

name: SAP and BigQuery: Instant insights with zero-copy and zero cost

speakers.jobTitle: Senior Principal of Product Strategy Operations

name: How Go code gets faster and more efficient over time

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, Infrastructure Architects & Admins

Learn how Go’s simple, readable syntax is ideal for AI agents and AI-generated code. Discover how to build a foundation that gets more efficient over time, with code as friendly to machines as it is to humans.

You'll learn how to transform your digital storefront into a proactive, frictionless shopping companion that redefines convenience and builds profound brand loyalty.

name: Boost your resilience posture with native fault injection testing

custom_fields.c_92133: DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins, Security Professionals

Plus, learn firsthand from two customers about their journeys and lessons from building mission-critical applications on Google Cloud.

name: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers

description: Move beyond simple prototypes and build agents that can access your organization’s most critical data – whether it lives in third-party SaaS or legacy on-premises systems. We’ll cover the full builder’s journey – from discovering and integrating with existing Google and partner Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers to building custom MCP servers from scratch using the specification boost add-on to instantly transform any API into agent-ready tools.

name: Accelerate your most demanding AI workloads with Managed Lustre

description: Don't let storage input/output starve your AI and ML workloads. Some of our successful customers will share how they use Managed Lustre for purposes ranging from high performance computing to AI. name: Build production-ready agents on Google Cloud: A guide for architects and CTOs

custom_fields.c_92133: Application Developers, Infrastructure Architects & Admins, IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: If you’re an IT leader, architect, or CTO tasked with moving AI from a “cool demo” to a core business asset, this session is for you. We’ll dive deep into top agent design patterns and the build vs. buy tradeoffs that every architect must navigate. name: Intelligent compute infrastructure: Design for performance, reliability, and cost

custom_fields.c_92133: Infrastructure Architects & Admins, IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: Unlock the full potential of Google compute power for all your applications, including the most challenging AI use cases. Join this session for actionable strategies and best practices to optimize cost, reliability, and management for cloud-first, AI, machine learning, high performance computing, and enterprise/stateful workloads. We’ll share recently released features to maximize ROI for each specific application type related to architecture decisions, configuration choices, and optimizations across multiple compute products, including AI Hypercomputer, Compute Engine, and Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE).

speakers.jobTitle: Director, Product Management, Google Compute Engine

name: Zero-ops GKE: How Palo Alto Networks composes secure, scalable systems

custom_fields.c_92133: DevOps, IT Ops, Platform Engineers, SREs, Infrastructure Architects & Admins, IT Managers & Business Leaders

description: Scaling distributed systems requires shifting from manual management to automated orchestration – a transition Palo Alto Networks has mastered. Join this session to go inside the architectural choices powering their high-performance environments. We’ll explore Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) Autopilot for a near-zero-ops experience and the technical implementation of horizontal pod autoscaling (HPA) to handle traffic without over-provisioning. We’ll also pull back the curtain on how they used Workload Identity Federation to replace static secrets. Leave with a real-world blueprint for composing scalable, secure, and lean systems.

name: Supporting large scale AI workloads with MIG's (Turnup and IMEX Config)
description: Join us for an unfiltered look at building massive-scale AI clusters outside of managed services (GKE). We will begin by level-setting on core GCP VM and GPU architecture, covering the specific turnup requirements for advanced A3/A4+ nodes as part of MIGs. We will share the business constraints that led us to bypass GKE, built to serve complex, multi-node inference and other demanding workloads. Finally, we'll explore the job scheduling strategies essential for running large-scale workloads on this bespoke platform.

name: GenAI for architects: Supercharge your workflow from blueprint to biz case
description: Enterprise Architects face technical debt and the struggle to align intricate designs with business strategy. GenAI offers a path to transform these hurdles into productivity gains. This session focuses on practical tools to enhance your architectural workflow, moving past theory to show how LLMs serve as "co-pilots" for automating time-intensive tasks. Learn to leverage GenAI to convert system designs into actionable strategies. Shift your focus from documentation maintenance to building a future-proof, high-velocity enterprise.

name: Agentic AI in action for financial services and insurance
description: The Financial Services and Insurance (FSI) industry faces pivotal challenges in data unification, risk management, and personalized experiences. While AI’s promise is clear, many struggle to move from concept to reality. This session explores how Agentic AI provides tangible solutions across capital markets and banking. We demonstrate precisely how to leverage Google's Agentic Transformation Framework to turn your vision into an operational reality. Learn to scale agents that address pressing FSI issues while maintaining strict compliance.

speakers.jobTitle: Industry Solutions Lead, Financial Services and Insurance
